Practice Management

best practice management software therapists

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

best practice management software therapists

You became a therapist to help people, not to chase unpaid claims or re-enter the same client data for the third time. Yet here you are—juggling calendars, HIPAA-compliant video, insurance portals, and note templates that don’t line up with DSM-5 criteria. The right practice-management system can give you those hours back. Below I compare the three tools most solo practitioners still narrow it down to in 2025: SimplePractice, TherapyNotes, and TheraNest. I’ve used all of them in real clinics on two continents; the notes below are what I wish someone had told me before I swiped the credit card.

Quick verdict: SimplePractice is the most complete, future-proof platform if you want everything in one login; TherapyNotes wins on price and clinical speed; ThaNest is the cheapest but is quietly riding into the sunset.

SimplePractice

Pricing

Starter US$39, Essential US$59, Plus US$79. Telehealth is included on Essential and up; on Starter it is a $15 add-on.

Core features

  • Full client portal (scheduling, intake, secure messaging, auto-billing)
  • Native iOS & Android apps that mirror the web view
  • In-house clearinghouse for US/CA; Stripe integrations for UK/AU
  • 100+ editable progress-note templates and treatment-plans
  • HIPAA-compliant telehealth with no time limits

Pros

  • Only option that lets you keep client data in the EU (Frankfurt) or Australia (Sydney) for GDPR compliance
  • Automated eligibility checks across 1,600+ US/CA payers
  • Google Calendar, Zapier, Stripe, Full Slate, Slack integrations
  • ERA/EFT enrollment and CAQH integration built in

Cons

  • Price jumps 30% once you add telehealth or insurance tools
  • No phone support on Starter or Essential—chat and email only
  • Learning curve is steeper; expect to spend a weekend setting up templates

Best for

Solo therapists who plan to add clinicians later or need non-US data residency.

TherapyNotes

Pricing

Flat US$49 for one clinician; each additional clinician is +US$30.

Core features

  • 200+ note templates pre-loaded with DSM-5 criteria
  • Built-in supervisor sign-off for interns & associates
  • Unlimited document storage and custom intake forms
  • HIPAA-compliant telehealth (browser-based)

Pros

  • Fastest note-writing workflow; auto-pulls treatment-plan goals
  • Live US-based phone support on every plan
  • Lowest total cost if you file insurance
  • ERA for Medicare, Medicaid, and major commercial payers

Cons

  • No native mobile app; browser on phone works but feels cramped
  • Client portal does not allow clients to schedule or start telehealth in the same window
  • Data hosted in US-East only—UK/EU practices need extra GDPR paperwork

Best for

Solo clinicians who live inside their documentation and want the cheapest reliable insurance billing.

TheraNest

Pricing

US$38 solo; US$48 first clinician + US$28 each additional.

Core features

  • Simple calendar, notes, and credit-card processing
  • Unlimited non-clinical staff logins (billing assistant, VA)
  • HIPAA-compliant telehealth (browser)

Pros

  • Cheapest month-to-month plan with no setup fee
  • Interface is light and fast—good for part-time or tech-averse therapists
  • Group practice scaling is the least expensive of the three

Cons

  • Product is in maintenance mode; new features roll into Therapy Brands’ unified platform instead
  • No local data hosting outside the US
  • PHI can’t be edited after 24-hour lock without support ticket
  • No Zapier, no two-way Google Calendar sync

Best for

Cash-pay, part-time therapists who want the lowest possible bill and don’t mind missing bells and whistles.

Head-to-head comparison

FeatureSimplePracticeTherapyNotesTheraNest
Starting monthly price (1 clinician)US$39US$49US$38
Telehealth includedYes (HIPAA)Yes (HIPAA)Yes (HIPAA)
Native mobile appiOS & AndroidNo (mobile site)No (mobile site)
Note templates100+ editable200+ DSM-5 aligned50+ basic
Client portalFull scheduling + intakeMessaging onlyMessaging only
Credit-card rate2.95% + 30¢2.9% + 30¢2.9% + 30¢
Data hosting regionsUS, EU, AUUS onlyUS only
Free trial30 days30 days21 days

Who should choose which

Choose SimplePractice if you want one login that still works when you add a second clinician, you need GDPR or Australian data residency, or you rely on Google Calendar and Zapier automations.
Choose TherapyNotes if documentation speed is your top priority, you file US insurance, and you can live without a mobile app.
Choose TheraNest if you run a Friday-only practice, take only private-pay, and want the absolute smallest monthly expense—just know you’ll migrate later when Therapy Brands retires the brand.

FAQ

What is the best practice management software for therapists who file Medicare claims?

TherapyNotes gives you ERA for Medicare and Medicaid at the lowest price; SimplePractice adds automatic eligibility checks if you see high volumes.

Can UK therapists use SimplePractice for GDPR-compliant notes?

Yes. SimplePractice hosts EU client data in Frankfurt and will sign a DPA; TherapyNotes and TheraNest require extra paperwork because they store only in the US.

Is there a free version of TherapyNotes or SimplePractice?

No, but both offer 30-day free trials—no credit card for TherapyNotes, card required for SimplePractice.

Which software has the fastest therapy progress-note templates?

TherapyNotes auto-pulls DSM-5 criteria and treatment-plan goals; most users finish notes in under two minutes.

Will TheraNest still exist in 2026?

Therapy Brands is migrating users to its unified platform; expect sunset incentives rather than new features.

Conclusion

If you’re still using paper or duct-taping three apps together, any of these three platforms will buy back your evenings. Pick SimplePractice for the most headroom, TherapyNotes for the lowest cost and fastest notes, or TheraNest for the smallest monthly hit while it lasts. Start a free trial, migrate your client list, and reclaim the time you spent wrestling technology instead of doing therapy.

Ready to stop admin work from eating your evenings?
